analysis: Ablation with alpha=0.0 (purely taxonomic similarity) and beta=1.0. Achieved 74.93% validation accuracy. This is better than the baseline (74.49%) but significantly worse than purely visual (75.65%) or fusion (75.30%). It confirms that taxonomic tree distance serves as a valid regularizer for fine-grained confusion, but it is less precise and less expressive than deep visual features.
